channel.title: World
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from World, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Prospects for U.S.-Iran Talks Rise as Tehran's Foreign Minister Travels to Region
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi is set to visit Islamabad, Muscat, and Moscow, as European officials express cautious optimism about renewed diplomatic engagement with the U.S.

item.title: Justice Department Watchdog to Investigate Handling of Epstein File Release
item.description: An internal DOJ watchdog will review how the agency managed the release of records related to Jeffrey Epstein, including whether relevant documents were appropriately redacted.

item.title: Trump Threatens Large Tariff on UK If Digital Services Tax Is Not Dropped
item.description: President Trump accused the United Kingdom of unfairly targeting American technology companies with its digital services tax, threatening new tariffs if the policy is not repealed.

item.title: Berlin Culture Minister Resigns Following Audit Into Antisemitism Fund Payments
item.description: An audit found that Culture Minister Sarah Wedl-Wilson approved public funds for organizations that had not undergone full vetting, prompting her resignation.

item.title: UK Assisted Dying Bill Fails to Pass After Running Out of Parliamentary Time
item.description: The assisted dying bill cleared two votes in the House of Commons but did not receive a vote in the Lords before the parliamentary session concluded.
